segments.text: You are also to make an altar of acacia wood for the burning of incense.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: It is to be square, a cubit long, a cubit wide, and two cubits high. Its horns must be of one piece.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Overlay with pure gold the top and all the sides and horns, and make a molding of gold around it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And make two gold rings below the molding on opposite sides to hold the poles used to carry it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Place the altar in front of the veil that is before the ark of the Testimony—before the mercy seat that is over the Testimony—where I will meet with you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And Aaron is to burn fragrant incense on it every morning when he tends the lamps.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: When Aaron sets up the lamps at twilight, he must burn the incense perpetually before the LORD for the generations to come.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: On this altar you must not offer unauthorized incense or a burnt offering or grain offering; nor are you to pour a drink offering on it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Once a year Aaron shall make atonement on the horns of the altar. Throughout your generations he shall make atonement on it annually with the blood of the sin offering of atonement. The altar is most holy to the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then the LORD said to Moses,
When you take a census of the Israelites to number them, each man must pay the LORD a ransom for his life when he is counted. Then no plague will come upon them when they are numbered.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Everyone who crosses over to those counted must pay a half shekel, according to the sanctuary shekel, which weighs twenty gerahs. This half shekel is an offering to the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Everyone twenty years of age or older who crosses over must give this offering to the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: In making the offering to the LORD to atone for your lives, the rich shall not give more than a half shekel, nor shall the poor give less.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Take the atonement money from the Israelites and use it for the service of the Tent of Meeting. It will serve as a memorial for the Israelites before the LORD to make atonement for your lives.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And the LORD said to Moses,
You are to make a bronze basin with a bronze st and for washing. Set it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and put water in it,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: with which Aaron and his sons are to wash their hands and feet.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Whenever they enter the Tent of Meeting or approach the altar to minister by presenting an offering made by fire to the LORD, they must wash with water so that they will not die.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Thus they are to wash their hands and feet so that they will not die; this shall be a permanent statute for Aaron and his descendants for the generations to come.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Then the LORD said to Moses,
Take the finest spices: 500 shekels of liquid myrrh, half that amount (250 shekels) of fragrant cinnamon,250 shekels of fragrant cane,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: 500 shekels of cassia—all according to the sanctuary shekel—and a hin of olive oil.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Prepare from these a sacred anointing oil, a fragrant blend, the work of a perfumer; it will be a sacred anointing oil.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Use this oil to anoint the Tent of Meeting, the ark of the Testimony,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: the table and all its utensils, the lampst and and its utensils, the altar of incense,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils, and the basin with its st and.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You are to consecrate them so that they will be most holy. Whatever touches them shall be holy.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anoint Aaron and his sons and consecrate them to serve Me as priests.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: And you are to tell the Israelites, ‘This will be My sacred anointing oil for the generations to come.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: It must not be used to anoint an ordinary man, and you must not make anything like it with the same formula. It is holy, and it must be holy to you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Anyone who mixes perfume like it or puts it on an outsider shall be cut off from his people.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The LORD also said to Moses,
Take fragrant spices—gum resin, onycha, galbanum, and pure frankincense—in equal measures,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: and make a fragrant blend of incense, the work of a perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Grind some of it into fine powder and place it in front of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting, where I will meet with you. It shall be most holy to you.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: You are never to use this formula to make incense for yourselves; you shall regard it as holy to the LORD.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Anyone who makes something like it to enjoy its fragrance shall be cut off from his people.
segments.speaker: the Lord
